goal: Clarify whether the AI Execution Agent replaces or supplements the rule-based FillWatcher. Document the decision as an Architecture Decision Record.

execution_agent.timing: Runs ONCE before order placement in execute_buy()
decisions: fill_watcher_params: per-trade overrides for the FillWatcher (timing, step, ceiling)
execution_agent.fallback: _fallback_plan() returns static CONFIG['fill_watcher'] values on any error — execution agent failure never blocks a trade

fill_watcher.timing: Runs CONTINUOUSLY after order placement until filled or cancelled
fill_watcher.decisions: None — it executes the plan set by the execution agent

call_sequence: 2. Place IBKR order using exec_plan.order_type + exec_plan.limit_price
findings.race_condition: None possible — execution agent finishes before order is placed; FillWatcher starts after

decision.rationale: The two systems are not alternatives — they are sequential stages of the same pipeline. The execution agent is the brain (strategic, one-shot); the FillWatcher is the mechanical arms (procedural, ongoing). Disabling either would break order execution.
decision.active_systems: Both active. Execution Agent configures; FillWatcher executes.

changes: ADR-001 comment block added to execution_agent.py (after module docstring)
Architecture note cross-referencing ADR-001 added to fill_watcher.py (after module docstring)
BACK-015 resolved entry added to backlog.json documenting the decision
Note: no duplicate 'Execution Agent' entries found in current backlog.json — the session goal assumed they existed, but they do not. BACK-015 is the definitive single entry.
